person.name: Konrad Rzentarzewski
person.bio: I was there when the config burned. In the age before observability, chaos reigned. The servers howled, and nothing scaled but entropy. Then kube cattle were born — simple, nameless beasts born of children’s games. But they multiplied. And their hunger grew vast. Years passed. The sun revolved the Earth. Then the Earth revolved the Cloud. I grew tired of debugging in darkness. I longed for light. And logs. Preferably structured. That’s when I began preaching — not loudly, just… declaratively. One apply at a time. I walked among DevOps tribes, where bash begat shell, and shell begat pain. To them I brought ConfigMaps, and Secrets hidden in plain sight. Then came the mysterious YAML — a language too holy to lint — inscribed deep within the API’s own heart. Through toil and merge, the infrastructure became manifest. Pods floated on the waters. Deployments begat replicas. Helm bore Charts. Git begat Ops. What was once dev spun to prod. And every service had a name — and every name resolved. Unless external DNS was involved. And the Preachers said: \"Behold, our logs streameth clean.\" Thus began the age of the declarative. We once dwelled in darkness. Now, we scale planetary in full light.

My aim is to minimize your risks by setting standards on software architecture and quality, deployment strategies, pipelines, and automation. I run the AWS User Group Frankfurt as a co-leader.

person.name: Marián Macháček
person.bio: I am a 30-year-old father of two daughters, currently working as a Senior DevOps Engineer and also serving as a deputy Tech Lead and Architect. As part of the IaaS squad for the Open Sovereign Cloud project, I am dedicated to advancing digital sovereignty in data, operations, and technology, with a strong focus on secure and private innovation—including confidential computing. My key skills include Linux, networking, containerization, orchestration, and hardware security modules (HSM), with hands-on experience in technologies such as Kubernetes, FluxCD, Prometheus, Grafana, Cilium, SONiC and many more.

person.name: Pramoda Kumar Jena
person.bio: I work as a Support Escalation Engineer at Microsoft, focusing on Kubernetes and container-related services, including Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS), Azure Container Registry (ACR), Azure Container Instance (ACI)and Azure Red Hat OpenShift (ARO). I bring extensive experience in cloud technologies and services, coupled with a strong background in Linux system administration and enterprise infrastructure support from my previous roles at AstraZeneca, IBM, and HCL.

person.name: Suyash Gupta
person.bio: With over 13 years of experience in cloud infrastructure and platform engineering, I currently serve as the Containers & Kubernetes Practice Lead within the Accenture AWS Business Group (AABG), driving the adoption of modern cloud-native technologies and Kubernetes across enterprise clients in retail, banking, and public sector domains. I’ve led notable Kubernetes transformations that have been showcased at AWS Summit London 2022. I’m deeply passionate about Kubernetes and Platform Engineering, specializing in building scalable cloud-native platforms for microservices and Data & AI workloads. I champion the “everything as code” philosophy while mentoring engineering teams to deliver resilient, automated, and developer-centric platform solutions. I’m always excited to connect with fellow Kubernetes enthusiasts and discuss cloud-native architectures—let’s talk more about K8s!

person.name: Amadou Ndiaye Dioum
person.bio: IT Project Manager and Cloud Native Specialist at BCEAO (Central Bank of West African States), where I lead the containerization and Kubernetes adoption for critical payment infrastructure serving 50+ banking participants across 8 West African countries. With all five Kubernetes certifications (CKA, CKAD, CKS, KCNA, KCSA) and over 10 years of experience in software development and system architecture, I specialize in building secure, observable, and resilient financial systems using Kubernetes, Helm, Prometheus, Grafana, and Trivy, alongside Docker Swarm, ELK stack, and Keycloak for enterprise identity management. I'm passionate about advancing Cloud Native adoption across the African fintech ecosystem and bringing enterprise-grade container orchestration expertise to the region.
